purpose: Reusable copy-and-adapt surface patterns for generated iOS app foundation work.
usageRule: Treat these as implementation references, not finished product screens. Every generated app must adapt copy, animation subject, benefits, CTA labels, package names, and routing to its PRD and UX.

patterns.purpose: Three-step onboarding that explains the promise, shows the product mechanic, and routes directly into first value.
useWhen: The app needs a premium but lightweight first-launch flow.
The first-value moment can be reached within one clear action after onboarding.
The product differentiator is explainable through a short visual sequence.
requiredAdaptation: Replace all copy with domain-specific promise, mechanic, and CTA text.
Replace the animation subject with an app-specific Lottie, SF Symbol animation, or native microinteraction.
Map the final CTA to the first-value entry point named in UX.
Use app-specific accessibility labels and stable UI test identifiers.
implementationNotes: Keep onboarding to 2-3 cards unless the PRD explicitly requires setup.
Do not ask for accounts, notifications, or purchase decisions before first value unless required by the approved topology.
Persist completion locally and keep a reset route available for QA.

patterns.purpose: Upgrade shell with a focused headline, app-specific benefit stack, package selector, CTA, restore, and legal routes.
useWhen: The first release needs an honest monetization shell before purchase integration is complete.
The app has a clear premium promise but RevenueCat or StoreKit wiring may be deferred.
The app needs screenshot-ready upgrade framing for experience review.
requiredAdaptation: Name the premium outcome in the headline; do not use generic 'Go Pro' copy.
Use benefits that match implemented or explicitly deferred premium capabilities.
Mark packages as mock, disabled, or wired according to runtime topology.
Wire restore, terms, privacy, and close routes even when purchase is deferred.
implementationNotes: The shell can be non-purchasing in foundation, but it must not look broken or dishonest.
If purchases are deferred, CTA copy must say what happens now, such as joining a waitlist or previewing premium.
Move real product identifiers and RevenueCat work into core or ops tasks only when approved by stories.
